title: 顺水推舟
summary:
Following a cynical discussion with an inspector regarding the motives for a woman to pretend not to recognize her husband, Hercule Poirot walks through a marketplace in St. Agatha. He observes the local architecture, noting the contrast between a small Roman Catholic church and the imposing Protestant St. Mary's statue. Inside the Catholic church, Poirot encounters Rosaline Clowes, who is sobbing. 

Rosaline expresses terror, claiming that David Hunter has been taken by authorities and that people want her dead. She insists David is innocent of the murder. When Poirot questions her about the lies she has told and asks if the deceased man was her husband, Robert Andrich, Rosaline becomes defensive and suspicious. She insists the dead man does not look like her husband and refuses to describe her husband's appearance before fleeing the church. Poirot, seemingly satisfied by her reaction, leaves the church and meets Rolly Claud and Lynn Marchmont at a building called Stagg. Rolly expresses gratitude for Poirot's recent assistance, though Poirot notes that Lynn appears strained. During their conversation, it is revealed that Lynn and Rolly have been engaged for six years, though Lynn has been serving overseas in the Royal Navy Women's Service.

Despite Rolly's annoyance, Poirot announces his intention to stay in the Wormsley Valley for leisure. Lynn privately asks Poirot if he believes David Hunter is guilty, to which Poirot responds by asking for her opinion. After the couple departs, Poirot visits the home of Dr. Lionel Clowes (referred to as Dr. Lionel Croude). He is greeted by Auntie Casey, who appears melancholy and unwelcoming, eventually inviting him in to speak in a hoarse whisper.
